name: Klenty
what_it_does: Sales engagement and prospecting automation platform for SDR teams, offering multi-channel outreach cadences, CRM-native integrations, and intent-based triggers to automate and personalize prospect follow-up.
use_cases: Running automated multi-step email cadences with personalized content blocks and conditional branching
Integrating with HubSpot, Salesforce, or Pipedrive to auto-enroll prospects in sequences from CRM triggers
Using intent signals (website visits, email opens, job changes) to trigger timely outreach automatically
Managing SDR team performance with sequence analytics and rep-level reporting
Programmatically enrolling and managing prospects in cadences via REST API from external tools
not_for: Small teams or individual sellers on tight budgets — Klenty is designed for SDR teams of 5+
Pure cold email volume play without CRM integration — Instantly or Snov.io offer simpler, cheaper options
Enterprise organizations needing revenue intelligence or call coaching (Outreach is better positioned)
Marketing teams running newsletters or broadcast emails to opted-in lists
best_when: A mid-market SDR team with an existing CRM (HubSpot, Salesforce, or Pipedrive) wants deep CRM-native sequence automation with intent-based triggering.
avoid_when: You don't have a CRM or don't need CRM integration — Klenty's differentiation is largely CRM-native workflow depth.

auth.notes: API key authentication passed as a header (api-key). Single key per account with no scope granularity — full read/write access. API keys are generated in the Klenty dashboard under Settings > API. No expiration or rotation controls.

paid_tiers.name: Startup
paid_tiers.notes: Email cadences, basic integrations, billed annually

paid_tiers.name: Pro
paid_tiers.notes: AI features, advanced reporting, priority support

pricing.notes: Annual billing required for stated prices — monthly billing is higher. 14-day free trial available. Minimum team sizes may apply. CRM integration features vary by plan.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses use basic JSON with a message field. HTTP status codes are generally correct (400, 401, 404, 429) but error messages are often too brief to diagnose the root cause. No structured error code taxonomy documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency key support. Prospect enrollment calls are not idempotent — agents must check enrollment status before calling to prevent duplicate cadence entries.
known_agent_gotchas: Cadences must be in active status before prospects can be enrolled — agents must check cadence status or handle enrollment failure gracefully
CRM field mapping is configured in the UI — agents cannot dynamically define field mappings via API
Intent triggers (job changes, website visits) are platform-managed and cannot be artificially fired via API for testing
Prospect status transitions (active, paused, completed, opted-out) must be tracked by agents — no event stream for status changes without webhooks
Annual contract requirement means platform changes cannot be easily exited — evaluate carefully before committing
LinkedIn steps require a connected LinkedIn account per user seat — agents cannot use LinkedIn automation without per-user setup
API documentation is limited and lacks examples for many endpoints — community support and sales team are primary documentation fallback
Webhook delivery failures are not retried — missed webhooks result in silent gaps in event tracking

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Single unscoped API key with full account access is the primary auth weakness. No scope controls, key rotation, or expiration. GDPR DPA available on request. CAN-SPAM and CCPA compliance features built into platform (unsubscribe handling, opt-out lists). Annual contracts include data processing terms.
